data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-66c98cde">
    <title>work: keep provider outages retryable instead of triage-parked</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
A transient route/provider outage parked 23 open beads behind `triage:needs-investigation`. Their latest actionable event was `execute-bead summary=execution_failed` with detail/body `execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found`. Once routing recovered, these beads remained excluded from `ddx work` until an operator manually removed the triage label.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:103-108 already classifies `no viable harness`, `no harness configured`, `failed to initialize routing service`, and `no viable provider` as FailureModeNoViableProvider.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:496-499 and cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go:137-142 explicitly exclude `no viable provider` from rate-limit retry handling, so this path does not get the bounded HTTP-429 retry treatment.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1191-1195 runs post-attempt triage for non-preserved execution failures and can then suppress progress with cooldown/triage metadata. In the observed queue, that produced durable `triage:needs-investigation` labels for transient provider outages.

PROPOSED FIX
Handle FailureModeNoViableProvider as a retryable transport outage, not a needs-investigation condition. A no-viable-provider attempt should unclaim the bead and either set a short retry cooldown or leave it retryable according to the existing retry policy, but it must not add or preserve `triage:needs-investigation` solely because routing was unavailable. `ddx work` diagnostics should distinguish provider outage from human-investigation blockers.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go and reconcile tests if stale labels need cleanup semantics

NON-SCOPE
- Do not treat quota exhaustion as HTTP 429 rate-limit retry; keep the current rate-limit contract boundary.
- Do not remove `triage:needs-investigation` for genuine `status: needs_investigation` no_changes rationales.
- Do not change Fizeau routing or model selection.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProviderDoesNotAddNeedsInvestigation verifies an execution_failed report classified as FailureModeNoViableProvider leaves the bead open/retryable without `triage:needs-investigation`.
2. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProviderUsesRetryableTransportPolicy verifies the bead is unclaimed and either has the intended short retry cooldown or is immediately retryable per the chosen policy; it must not be parked as human investigation.
3. TestReconcile_NoViableProviderClearsStaleNeedsInvestigation verifies a bead whose latest event is no-viable-provider has stale `triage:needs-investigation` removed by reconcile/cleanup if that path owns cleanup.
4. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NeedsInvestigationRationaleStillParks verifies genuine `status: needs_investigation` no_changes behavior is unchanged.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProvider|TestReconcile_NoViableProvider|TestExecuteBeadWorker_NeedsInvestigationRationaleStillParks" -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:routing, area:beads, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="c13cd40154680bd3f6a85043ab67464237bd1a1b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T234219-527d9fed"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed `ae61e08a3` (`fix(bead): keep provider outages retryable [ddx-66c98cde]`).

What changed:
- `cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go` now recognizes a latest `execute-bead` `execution_failed` event with `no_viable_provider` and removes stale `triage:needs-investigation` instead of preserving it. See [reconcile.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T234219-527d9fed/cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go#L128) and the classifier helper at [reconcile.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T234219-527d9fed/cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go#L252).
- Added exact-name acceptance tests in [provider_outage_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T234219-527d9fed/cli/internal/bead/provider_outage_test.go#L16) covering:
  - provider outages bypass post-attempt triage and stay retryable,
  - genuine `status: needs_investigation` no_changes behavior still parks.
- Added reconcile coverage in [reconcile_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T234219-527d9fed/cli/internal/bead/reconcile_test.go#L105).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProvider|TestReconcile_NoViableProvider|TestExecuteBeadWorker_NeedsInvestigationRationaleStillParks" -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

Note: this environment needs `CGO_ENABLED=1` for the repo’s `go-test` hook because it runs `go test -race`.
